transcript: Speaker A: I left my village and came to this city. Leaving my father, my mother, my home behind. People said I won't succeed. They said, 'English is not for someone like you.' But I refused to believe them. Because I carry something they can't see. My father's sacrifice. My mother's prayers. Their tired hands. Their sleepless nights. All of it... for me. Some days, the city feels too loud. The language feels too foreign. I miss home. I miss them. But then I remember why I left. I open MySivi. Just 10 minutes. The AI teacher doesn't judge me. Every mistake? A lesson. Every session? One step closer to them. I'm not competing with this city. I'm just better than I was yesterday. When doubt creeps in, I open the app. When loneliness hits at midnight, I press start. Because I made a promise — to them. One day soon, I'll call my father with confidence. I'll walk into that interview room with my head high. I'll send money home. I'll make them proud. Not because I was the smartest. Not because it was easy. But because I didn't give up. MySivi didn't just teach me English. It reminded me who I'm doing this for. Mummy, Papa... Your son is still trying. And your son is not done yet. I will practice on MySivi everyday and make you proud. Speaker B: इन्स्टॉल बटन पर क्लिक करो और MySivi ऐप डाउनलोड करो। [Click on the install button and download the MySivi app.]

summary: An emotional ad featuring a hand highlighting a printed poem about a young man's journey from his village to the city. He uses the MySivi app to practice English daily to honor his parents' sacrifices and build a better future. The ad ends with a Hindi voiceover prompting viewers to download the app.
